paragraphs: A ferryman dies on his own bridge with a satchel meant for the dead still warm in the ropes. The road beyond opens toward a frontier conspiracy built from survey maps, church pressure, and a ruin that has started answering to names no living scholar should know.

To follow the satchel is to move through Ambrian institutions that want the truth controlled rather than found. Every clue asks whether the protagonist can expose what happened to Elira without becoming one more servant of the ruin beneath the watchtower.
central_conflict: The protagonist must uncover who turned a buried ruin into a living source of leverage before the people controlling it complete an opening they cannot close.
tone_statement: Patient, rain-soaked dark fantasy with sharp bursts of violence and a constant sense that every truth has a moral cost.
